title: Banksia cinerea
taxonomy: Banksia cinerea is a species of flowering plant in the family Proteaceae. It is native to Western Australia. The plant is classified as a member of the genus Banksia and the order Proteales. Within the genus, Banksia cinerea is a part of the series Cyrtostylis. The species is further classified within the subsection Cyrtostylis.

intro: Banksia cinerea, also known as the hairy-leaved banksia, is a species of shrub native to Western Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine, with its bark, leaves, and seeds exhibiting antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, offering potential health benefits including wound healing, fever reduction, and protection against oxidative stress. Bioactive constituents of Banksia cinerea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenes, which have been identified as the main contributors to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Banksia cinerea include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support immune function, soothe respiratory issues, and promote overall well-being.
